LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR TEMPORARY RESTRAINING ORDER AND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE COURT PROVIDES COUNSEL WITH A TENTATIVE RULING PRIOR TO THE HEARING. THE MATTER IS ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE APPLICATION IS DENIED. THE COURT WILL ISSUE AN ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR 5976

LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO ANSWER. NO APPEARANCES IN THE MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. DEFENDANT DEMURRED TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE AND PLAINTIFF DISMISSED THAT CLAIM. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION VERIFIED PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. A R G U E D AND THE COURT RULES AS FOLLOWS: DENIED. THE COURT BELIEVES THAT THE USE OF FORCE ISSUE HAS BEEN CLEARLY DEFINED BY THE APPELLATE COURTS, AND THE CITY IS WITHIN ITS CONSTITUTIONAL AND CITY MANAGEMENT AUTHORITY TO EXERCISE LOCAL POLICE AUTHORITY IN REGARDS TO USE OF FORCE AS DEFINED IN SAN JOSE. IN ADDITION, THERE IS SOME EVIDENCE OF GOOD FAITH ON THE CITY'S PART IN REGARDS TO WORK CONDITIONS THAT MAY RESULT IN THE INSTITUTION OF THAT POLICY. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: NEWTON LAM; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On San Francisco Police Officers Association Verified Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gregg Adam, of Messing Adam & Jasmine LLP, Tel. 415.845.6517, for petitioners San Francisco Police Officers' Association;
Trevor Koski, of the Office of the City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3800, for respondents City and County of San Francisco.

Ruling: A R G U E D. PETITIONER SAN FRANCISCO POLICE OFFICERS' ASSOCIATION Verified Petition To Compel Arbitration is denied. The Court believes that the use of force issue has been clearly defined by the appellate courts, and the city is within its constitutional and city management authority to exercise local police authority in regards to use of force as defined in San Jose. In addition, there is some evidence of good faith on the city's part in regards to work conditions that may result in the institution of that policy. Prevailing party shall prepare form of order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

DEMURRER TO ANSWER
Off Calendar Feb-27-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances in the matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO ANSWER is taken off calendar as moot. Defendant demurred to the fourth cause of action for writ of mandate and plaintiff dismissed that claim. Judge: Newton Lam; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.

Hearing Re: Petitioner'S Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order And Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Dec-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Petitioner San Francisco Police Officers' Association's Ex Parte Application For Temporary Restraining Order and Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction

Appearances:
Gregg Adam of Messing Adam & Jasmine LLP (415.266.1801) for petitioner and moving party;
Katharine Hobin Porter of the Office of the City Attorney (415.554.3896) for respondents and opposing party

The Court provides counsel with a tentative ruling prior to the hearing. The matter is argued and the Court adopts its tentative ruling. The application is denied. The Court will issue an order. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR 5976
